behaviors.description: Generated reply cites specific points from the incoming email, not a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) output must be context-aware to a specific thread, (2) failure is generic/non-grounded wording. Hypothetical elsewhere: email API delivery, approval gating, KB retrieval outages. MOCK-MISS: this is not just a pure local function check; the failure is in model output grounding. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model can be forced to reference points and would hide grounding failures. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real LLM generation quality/variance. MULTI-STEP: no explicit multi-agentic chain in this behavior alone. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires generation quality for one step, not a multi-step agent pipeline assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on representative email threads; assert the draft includes thread-specific entities/decisions/questions from the input and fails if responses are generic despite correct prompt wiring.

behaviors.description: Factual consistency check flags draft claims that contradict the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) claims from draft must be compared to KB entries, (2) contradictions must be flagged. Hypothetical elsewhere: quality of LLM drafting, transport/email sending, UI approval. MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (draft claim extraction/comparison/flag propagation), not necessarily in one isolated function. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes for KB data still expose contradiction-detection wiring bugs. LLM-DEP: behavior text does not require real model quality judgments; it requires contradiction flagging against provided KB entries. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No from behavior text. (b) real AI/ML output quality needed? Not explicitly. (c) multiple real agentic steps? No. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about contradiction detection against KB entries, not model output relevance/quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft parser/claim extractor + KB lookup + consistency checker with fixture drafts/KB rows containing known contradictions; verify contradiction flags and surfaced metadata across module boundaries.

behaviors.description: If no relevant KB entries exist, the draft includes a disclaimer and does not fabricate facts.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) KB relevance can be empty, (2) generated text must include uncertainty/disclaimer, (3) fabricated facts are failure. Hypothetical elsewhere: approval send gate, external email transport. MOCK-MISS: single-function checks are insufficient because the key risk is model behavior under missing-context conditions. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is explicit. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs can always return a disclaimer and hide hallucination risk. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real generation behavior when evidence is absent. MULTI-STEP: this behavior does not require verifying the whole pipeline sequence. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text focuses on one generation condition (no relevant KB) rather than end-to-end pipeline completion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM generation with prompts where retrieval returns empty/irrelevant KB context; assert presence of explicit disclaimer language and absence of unsupported factual assertions in the draft.

behaviors.description: Sending is blocked until explicit user approval; no automatic send occurs.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) approval state is required before send action, (2) auto-send must not occur. Hypothetical elsewhere: LLM content quality, KB contradiction semantics, external SMTP variability. MOCK-MISS: this is a module-boundary policy enforcement (approval workflow -> sender). Unit tests of one function can miss wiring/state propagation bugs. REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for real email provider or OS primitives; a local fake sender can reveal premature send calls. MOCK-HIDE: mocking sender does not inherently hide this failure if call assertions are strict. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: not agentic pipeline validation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) OS/network/subprocess? No explicit requirement. (b) real AI/ML output? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about blocking logic before send, not real transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Test approval controller + state store + send service wiring: create draft, withhold approval, assert send service is not invoked; then provide explicit approval and assert single send invocation.

behaviors.description: End-to-end sequence (read thread, generate draft, fact-check, present for approval) works for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: explicit full-sequence validation across multiple stages, including an agentic generation step, on a real thread artifact. Hypothetical elsewhere: unrelated admin tooling, analytics, background jobs. MOCK-MISS: isolating modules misses cross-stage state/hand-off failures across the pipeline. REAL-INFRA: may involve real thread source and infrastructure, but the defining boundary here is full pipeline execution. LLM-DEP: yes, generation step depends on real model behavior. MULTI-STEP: yes, behavior explicitly requires the full agent pipeline sequence. Rejection gate: Agentic alone rejected because behavior text requires sequence integrity across read->generate->check->present, not only single-step model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full assistant pipeline on a real email thread fixture/source through all stages and assert stage outputs/hand-offs: thread parsed, draft generated, fact-check result attached, approval UI/state presented before any send path.
